headline: Medical Negligence Claims Rise 45% But Patients Face 7-Year Court Waits
context: Medical negligence litigation increased significantly while case resolution times stretched to an average of 7 years.

cards.big: Medical negligence claims rose 45% from 2022 to 2025 with average resolution taking 7 years
cards.sub: Patients face emotional and financial burden of prolonged litigation for justice

cards.big: Malaysia has no medical ombudsman or independent health complaints mechanism
cards.sub: Patients must choose between internal hospital complaints or expensive court litigation

cards.big: Only 22% of medical negligence plaintiffs receive any compensation after full litigation
cards.sub: Low success rates deter legitimate claims and deny justice to affected patients

cards.big: New Zealand's no-fault ACC system resolves medical injury claims within 6 months on average
cards.sub: No-fault approach provides faster compensation without requiring proof of negligence

cards.big: A 7-year wait for medical negligence justice is itself a form of institutional negligence
Should Malaysia establish a no-fault medical injury compensation scheme separate from courts
